name: El Casar, Valdepiélagos, and Algete Loop
name_original: El Casar, Valdepiélagos, Algete – ruta circular
description: Cycle a moderate 34.3-mile (55.1 km) loop through the ZEPA Estepas Cerealistas de los Ríos Jarama y Henares, with well-paved roads.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around El Casar traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and varied terrain in the province of Guadalajara, Spain. The region, situated at an elevation of 833 meters, offers accessible routes with gradients up to 4.5%. Cyclists can explore areas near the Jarama River and historical sites, providing both physical challenge and cultural interest. The terrain primarily consists of paved surfaces, suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around El Casar The most popular road cycling route is El Casar, Valdepiélagos, and Algete Loop, a 34.2 miles (55.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route features an elevation gain of 396 meters across rolling landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is El Casar, Algete, Fuente el Saz del Jarama – circular route, an easy 19.8 miles (31.8 km) path. This route offers a gentler ride through the local countryside, with an elevation gain of 297 meters. Local road cyclists also love the Valdepiélagos – Vadillo de Gonzalo loop from Av. Valdetorres - Islas Virgenes, a 30.9 miles (49.8 km) trail leading through the rural surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Road cycling around El Casar is defined by rolling hills, the Jarama River corridor, and glimpses of historical trails and towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore El Casar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around El Casar?
A: Road cycling routes in El Casar primarily feature rolling hills and varied terrain, with most surfaces being paved. You can expect climbs with gradients up to 4.5%, offering both scenic views and opportunities for a good workout.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in El Casar?
A: Yes, El Casar offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980819879/el-casar-algete-fuente-el-saz-del-jarama-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Casar, Algete, Fuente el Saz del Jarama – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 31.8 km path with a gentler elevation gain of 297 meters.

Q: What options are there for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenge?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, El Casar provides 4 difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40374481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of El Atazar Reservoir – Vadillo de Gonzalo loop from El Casar&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 105 km route with an elevation gain of nearly 1170 meters, offering significant climbs and rewarding descents.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in El Casar?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around El Casar are circular. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977458521/el-casar-valdepielagos-and-algete-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Casar, Valdepiélagos, and Algete Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.1 km moderate route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdepiélagos – Vadillo de Gonzalo loop from Av. Valdetorres - Islas Virgenes&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 49.8 km trail through rural surroundings.

Q: What are the typical road conditions for cycling in El Casar?
A: The road cycling routes in El Casar are predominantly on paved surfaces, making them suitable for road bikes. While some areas might have a small percentage of unpaved sections, the focus for road cycling is on well-maintained roads across the rolling landscapes.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in El Casar?
A: The best seasons for road cycling in El Casar are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is generally milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable and enjoyable rides.

Q: What landmarks and points of interest can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around El Casar offer glimpses of historical and natural landmarks. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4615324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Gate of Talamanca de Jarama&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7420419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viñuelas Mill&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3407915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge Over the Jarama River&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural feature.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in El Casar?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations for road cycling can vary, the easier routes in El Casar, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980819879/el-casar-algete-fuente-el-saz-del-jarama-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Casar, Algete, Fuente el Saz del Jarama – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, with its gentler profile, could be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with road cycling. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists in El Casar?
A: While specific dedicated cyclist parking facilities are not detailed, El Casar, as a municipality, offers various public parking options within the town center. It's advisable to look for general public parking areas, especially near the Plaza Mayor or other central points, from which you can easily start your cycling routes.

Q: What is the average length and duration of road cycling routes near El Casar?
A: The road cycling routes near El Casar vary significantly in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977458521/el-casar-valdepielagos-and-algete-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Casar, Valdepiélagos, and Algete Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 55.1 km and takes about 2 hours 20 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1312639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Cobeña – Lagunas de Cobeña loop from El Casar&lt;/a&gt; is 71.2 km. There are also longer, more challenging routes exceeding 100 km.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in El Casar pass through or near small towns and villages like Talamanca de Jarama, Valdepiélagos, and Algete. These locations typically offer cafes, bars, or small shops where you can stop for refreshments, water, or a quick meal. It's always a good idea to check the route map for towns along your chosen path.
tips: From this point there are spectacular views.
Winding stretch of road to enjoy leveling
Its parish church stands out, especially its main altarpiece, and also the La Tejera fountain.

Algete is a charming municipality in the Community of Madrid that offers a variety of activities and interesting places to visit.
Nuestra Señora de la Asunción Church: A historic church with stunning architecture.
Plaza de la Constitución: A great place to stroll and soak up the local atmosphere.
Additionally, Algete has a rich history and local traditions that you can explore.
In the surroundings you can see a wide variety of birds such as quails, partridges, bustards, blackbirds, wood pigeons and different types of birds of prey.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 55.1 kilometers long and, for a moderate road cyclist, it typically takes around 2 hours and 20 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: While classified as 'moderate,' this route requires good fitness due to its length and around 400 meters of elevation gain. The terrain is mostly well-paved, which makes it generally easy to ride, but beginners should be prepared for the distance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Casar, Valdepiélagos, Algete circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, so you can start and park in any of the towns it passes through, such as El Casar, Valdepiélagos, or Algete. Look for public parking areas within these towns to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride. You'll be on roads suitable for racebikes, with some gentle climbs and descents adding to the moderate challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse scenery typical of the Madrid region, including fertile plains, views of the Jarama river, and potentially distant glimpses of the Guadarrama mountain range. The route often passes through picturesque villages and areas with olive groves and vineyards.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be very hot in the Madrid region, and winters can be chilly, especially if there are distant snow-capped peaks in view.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several points of interest. You'll pass by the Viewpoint of El Casar, ride along the Road Between El Casar and the Junction to Valdenuño, and go through settlements like Vadillo de Gonzalo and Algete Town Center. You can also see the Church of the Assumption of Our Lady of Valdeolmos.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several official trails. You'll encounter small sections of the Camino de Santiago Complutense, Camino Valdetorres, Senda del Colesterol Alalpardo-Valdeolmos, and Camino del Cubillo, among others.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs on a racebike route due to safety concerns for both the dog and the cyclist, especially given the distance and traffic.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the ZEPA Estepas Cerealistas de los Ríos Jarama y Henares?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, even though it passes through the ZEPA Estepas Cerealistas de los Ríos Jarama y Henares. However, as it's a protected area, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or supplies along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like El Casar, Valdepiélagos, and Algete, you will find opportunities to stop for water, snacks, or a meal. Algete Town Center, for instance, offers various amenities.
